karen packs peanut butter sandwiches in her daughter's lunch 2 randomly chosen days of the week. the other 3 school days, she packs a ham sandwich. she packs a piece of cake on one randomly chosen day and cookies the other four days. what is the probability, expressed as a percentage, that karen packs a ham sandwich and cake on the same day?

module 1: calculate the probability of karen packing a ham sandwich on a randomly chosen day. there are 5 days in a week. 2 days she packs peanut butter sandwiches, 3 days she packs ham sandwiches. so the probability of a ham sandwich is 3/5.

module 2: calculate the probability of karen packing a piece of cake on a randomly chosen day. there are 5 days in a week. 1 day she packs a piece of cake, 4 days she packs cookies. so the probability of cake is 1/5.

module 3: calculate the probability of karen packing both a ham sandwich and a piece of cake on the same day. this requires multiplying the probabilities of each event occurring on the same day. the probability of a ham sandwich is 3/5 and the probability of cake is 1/5. so the combined probability is (3/5) * (1/5) = 3/25.

module 4: convert the probability to a percentage. 3/25 is equal to 0.12, which is 12%.
